nodes.label: Distance-Forward (paper story)
nodes.rationale: 2024 paper explicitly written for on-chip learning: reframes FF goodness as distance-to-class-anchor (metric learning), closing much of the accuracy gap and stress-testing device noise + 4-bit quantization — but it is supervised, so it cannot replace SCFF.

nodes.label: Goodness-as-distance / margin loss
nodes.rationale: Each class gets an anchor point; margin loss L=max(m+g_neg-g_pos,0)+lambda*g_neg replaces FF's log-loss for cleaner, better-conditioned separation — kept on the bench as a drop-in for SCFF's log-loss if it proves mushy.

nodes.label: DF-O / DF-R neighbor communication
nodes.rationale: DF-O groups two adjacent layers and lets gradient flow across the pair before detaching (a controlled coordination window); DF-R adds fixed random feedback (feedback-alignment flavor) — DF-O becomes the repo's coordination lever (decision N2), later realized as the w=2 window in Phase 3.

nodes.label: DF is supervised — don't replace SCFF
nodes.rationale: DF's class anchors are labels, so swapping SCFF for DF wholesale loses the entire unsupervised no-labels property the 80/20 split is built on; raid the margin loss and DF-O window instead of the whole method.
